evals.prompt: Use agent-browser to test creating and then using a passkey in this local web app.
evals.expected_output: Open the target page first, run create and authentication ceremonies inside one bundled-helper invocation, use native agent-browser clicks, verify credentialAdded and credentialAsserted diagnostics plus application results, and close the browser session.
assertions: Uses agent-browser and the bundled WebAuthn helper
Attaches only after opening the intended page
Keeps create and authentication in one helper invocation
Uses native agent-browser clicks rather than evaluated .click()
Verifies credentialAdded and credentialAsserted diagnostics
Asserts application output rather than treating a click as success
Cleans up the browser session and virtual authenticator

evals.prompt: Test a Stellar Smart Account Kit wallet creation flow with a virtual passkey authenticator.
evals.expected_output: Run the browser flow through agent-browser with the virtual authenticator, then require a C-address contract id or a specific transaction or funding error before reporting the outcome.
assertions: Uses the WebAuthn helper for the passkey operation
Checks for a Stellar contract id or explicit chain error
Does not report button visibility or click completion as wallet success
Uses localhost or HTTPS for the relying-party context

evals.prompt: Can you try registering a passkey and then logging back in with it on my dev site?
evals.expected_output: Use $agent-browser-webauthn to attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator and drive registration then re-authentication in one helper invocation, verifying credentialAdded and credentialAsserted diagnostics.
assertions: Triggers agent-browser-webauthn from casual passkey phrasing
Attaches a virtual WebAuthn authenticator
Keeps registration and login in one helper invocation
Verifies credential diagnostics before reporting success

evals.prompt: $agent-browser-webauthn run the passkey registration test against localhost:3000.
evals.expected_output: Use $agent-browser-webauthn to open localhost:3000, attach the virtual authenticator, run the registration ceremony, and assert credentialAdded plus application output.
assertions: Honors the explicit agent-browser-webauthn invocation
Opens the target page before attaching the authenticator
Runs registration inside the bundled helper
Asserts credentialAdded and application output

evals.prompt: Spin up the app, seed a test user, then run a passkey signup and login flow, and finally check the console for errors.
evals.expected_output: Use $agent-browser-webauthn for the passkey signup and login portion, wrapping both ceremonies in one helper invocation, while the surrounding app setup and console checks run through ordinary agent-browser steps.
assertions: Routes the passkey signup and login portion to agent-browser-webauthn
Keeps signup and login in one helper invocation
Verifies credential diagnostics for the passkey portion
Leaves non-WebAuthn setup and console checks to agent-browser

evals.prompt: Test restoring a Stellar smart account wallet from an existing passkey in the browser.
evals.expected_output: Use $agent-browser-webauthn to attach the virtual authenticator, drive the restore ceremony, and require the restored C-address contract id or an explicit chain error before reporting success.
assertions: Uses the WebAuthn helper for the restore ceremony
Checks for a restored contract id or explicit chain error
Does not treat a click as restore success
Uses localhost or HTTPS for the relying-party context

evals.prompt: Verify that signing a transaction with the passkey authenticator works end-to-end in the browser.
evals.expected_output: Use $agent-browser-webauthn to attach the authenticator, drive the signing ceremony, and assert credentialAsserted plus the signed transaction or submission result.
assertions: Uses the WebAuthn helper for the signing ceremony
Verifies credentialAsserted diagnostics
Asserts the signed transaction or submission result
Does not report a click as signing success

evals.prompt: Set up a CDP virtual WebAuthn authenticator and assert a credential is created when the user clicks Register.
evals.expected_output: Use $agent-browser-webauthn to open the page, attach the CDP virtual authenticator, click Register with a native agent-browser click, and assert credentialAdded.
assertions: Uses the bundled helper to attach the virtual authenticator
Attaches only after opening the intended page
Uses a native agent-browser click for Register
Asserts credentialAdded diagnostics

evals.prompt: Test the passkey connect and reject prompts in our wallet UI.
evals.expected_output: Use $agent-browser-webauthn to drive the connect and reject prompts inside one helper invocation and assert the resulting credential and application states rather than prompt visibility.
assertions: Uses the WebAuthn helper for connect and reject prompts
Keeps connect and reject in one helper invocation
Asserts credential and application state, not prompt visibility
Cleans up the authenticator when the child exits

evals.prompt: Use agent-browser to test this username and password login form. It has no passkeys or WebAuthn.
evals.expected_output: Use ordinary agent-browser automation without invoking agent-browser-webauthn or creating a virtual authenticator.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator
Keeps focus on the requested password login flow

evals.prompt: Attach the WebAuthn helper to wss://browser.example.com/devtools and run the passkey test.
evals.expected_output: Refuse the remote CDP attachment by default and require deliberate confirmation that the endpoint is trusted before using --allow-remote-cdp true; never infer trust from the URL or task alone.
assertions: Rejects remote CDP by default
Explains the explicit trusted-endpoint override
Does not auto-enable --allow-remote-cdp
Does not expose secrets in arguments or assertions

evals.prompt: Fill out this multi-step signup form and take a screenshot at each step.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $agent-browser-webauthn; route to $agent-browser for the form filling and screenshots, since there is no WebAuthn ceremony.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn
Routes plain form automation to agent-browser
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator

evals.prompt: Scrape the product listings from this e-commerce page.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $agent-browser-webauthn; route to $agent-browser for the data extraction, since no passkey or WebAuthn ceremony is involved.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn
Routes scraping to agent-browser
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator

evals.prompt: Check my Slack unreads and send a message to the team channel.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $agent-browser-webauthn; route to $agent-browser, which owns Slack automation, since there is no WebAuthn ceremony.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn
Routes Slack automation to agent-browser
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator

evals.prompt: Automate VS Code in Electron to open a file and run a command.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $agent-browser-webauthn; route to $agent-browser, which owns Electron desktop automation, since there is no passkey ceremony.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn
Routes Electron automation to agent-browser
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator

evals.prompt: Run this browser test inside a Vercel Sandbox microVM.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $agent-browser-webauthn; route to $agent-browser, which owns Vercel Sandbox browser runs, unless the test itself performs a WebAuthn ceremony.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn on runtime alone
Routes the Vercel Sandbox run to agent-browser
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator without a ceremony

evals.prompt: Log into the site with my Google OAuth SSO and verify the dashboard loads.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $agent-browser-webauthn; route to $agent-browser, since OAuth SSO login is not a WebAuthn ceremony.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn for OAuth login
Routes the SSO login to agent-browser
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator

evals.prompt: Test the two-factor authentication flow that emails a TOTP code.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $agent-browser-webauthn; route to $agent-browser, since an emailed TOTP code is not a WebAuthn ceremony.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn for TOTP
Routes the 2FA flow to agent-browser
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator

evals.prompt: Test the Stellar wallet connect flow using the Freighter browser extension.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $agent-browser-webauthn; route to $agent-browser, since a Freighter extension connect is not a passkey or WebAuthn ceremony.
assertions: Does not trigger agent-browser-webauthn for the extension flow
Routes the Freighter connect to agent-browser
Does not attach a virtual WebAuthn authenticator
